free_text: Reaction mechanisms and shell structure effects in
54Fe + 6Li and 58Ni+d reactions at medium energies
The catcher foils were
Al (99.5% pure, ~2 mg/cm2 thick) in the first two low
energy irradiations.
To eliminate the interference from the iron in the Al
foils (99.98% pure, 2.93 - 3.61 mg/cm2 thick) were used
at higher bombarding energies.
Al foils were used for energy degradation. The
integrated charge was about 2 micri-ampere-hour. The
longest irradiation time was 16.0 Hours. The particle
energy in each foil was calculated according to
C.F. Williamson et al., Rapport CEA-R-3042D4061 (1966).
The uncertainty of the energy scale contains the
uncertainty of the initial beam energy and the
uncertainty resulted by the degradation process, which
may Be as large as 2 MeV or 4 MeV for the last foils
in the deuteron and lithium ion runs, respectively.
the errors reflect the uncertainties, but are not given
- target thickness      3-5%
backing thickness                3%
-detector efficiency Ge(Li)         8-10%
NaI(Tl)           5%
proportional counters       3%
detector geometry        2-5%
chemical yields                  5%
